title: Mycoplasma testing
description: Universal QC routine: antibiotic-free culture to 50 to 70%, then PCR / Hoechst / luminescence test. Source: ATCC kit 30-1012K.

payload.description: Universal mycoplasma screening for cell cultures. Grow cells WITHOUT antibiotics (antibiotics mask low-level mycoplasma; ATCC uses none routinely). Sample at 50 to 70% confluence; do not over-grow or trypsinize the sample. Adherent cells: scrape into the existing spent medium, do NOT use trypsin or EDTA (they disrupt mycoplasma). Use no more than 10^6 cells (10^4 to 10^5 are needed for the assay). Detection options: PCR (ATCC Universal Mycoplasma Detection Kit 30-1012K; a positive sample shows a band at 434 to 468 bp, the positive control 464 bp, the negative control none), Hoechst 33258 DNA stain, MycoAlert or LookOut luminescence, or direct agar culture (4 to 5 weeks). Test routinely: on receipt, about every 4 to 6 weeks, and before banking. Sources: ATCC kit 30-1012K https://www.atcc.org/products/30-1012k ; ATCC mycoplasma contamination https://www.atcc.org/the-science/authentication/mycoplasma-contamination

cell_line.name: Any cell line
cell_line.notes: Culture WITHOUT antibiotics before testing. Sample at 50 to 70% confluence. Sources: ATCC (see description).

media.base_medium: Complete growth medium, ANTIBIOTIC-FREE (antibiotics mask low-level mycoplasma)

planned_events.notes: Passage cells into ANTIBIOTIC-FREE complete medium and grow toward 50 to 70% confluence. Antibiotics mask low-level mycoplasma.

At 50 to 70% confluence, collect the sample. Adherent: scrape into the existing spent medium (no trypsin or EDTA). Use no more than 10^6 cells. For PCR, spin debris at 13,000 rpm for 5 min at 4 C, keep the supernatant, and run the ATCC Universal Mycoplasma Detection Kit (30-1012K). A positive sample shows a band at 434 to 468 bp.
Interpret the result. If positive, discard or decontaminate the culture and re-test. Repeat testing about every 4 to 6 weeks and before banking.
